pr.title: feat: add agent-runtime/ monorepo scaffold (shared brain + OpenCode/Aider/Pi adapters)
pr.body:
Introduces `agent-runtime/` — a self-contained monorepo layer that wires a single canonical "brain" (agents, skills, memory, MCP) into three runtime adapters (OpenCode, Aider, Pi) managed by Agent Deck as the cockpit.

## Canonical shared brain (`shared/`)
- **4 agent prompts** — Planner, Implementer, Reviewer, PI; each defines role, output format, and hard rules
- **Memory store** — `scratchpad.md` (ephemeral), `decisions.md` (ADR log + template), `glossary.md`
- **4 skill packs** with prompt templates — `memory/`, `code/`, `repo/`, `qa/`
- **MCP docs** — common server catalogue, env-var conventions

## Runtime adapters
| Adapter | Key files |
|---------|-----------|
| `opencode/` | `opencode.config.json` (4 sessions, 2 MCP servers wired) |
| `aider/` | `aider.conf.yml` (commented, minimal), `scripts/aider-run.sh` |
| `pi/` | `AGENTS.md` (Pi-native agent defs), `scripts/pi-run.sh` |

Adapter `agents/` and `skills/` directories are populated by sync, not symlinks (cross-platform).

## Agent Deck cockpit config (`agent-deck/`)
- `config.toml` — sessions, worktrees, MCP, Docker, remote placeholders
- `worktree-setup.sh` — creates `agent/{planner,implementer,reviewer,pi}` git worktrees for parallel work
- `conductors/README.md` — supervisor-agent pattern with example pipeline config

## Scripts (`scripts/`)
- `sync-skills.sh` — POSIX `sh`; plain `cp -r` (no symlinks) of `shared/agents` + `shared/skills` into every adapter; safe to re-run
- `run-{opencode,aider,pi}.sh` — loads `.env`, runs sync, checks binary, execs runtime
- `launch-deck.sh` — starts Agent Deck pointed at `agent-deck/config.toml`
- `run-all.sh` — starts all three adapters in background, then Agent Deck in foreground

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

Create an initial monorepo structure for “Agent Deck is the missing cockpit layer” runtime in the GitHub repository `Brucesquared2/agent-deck`.

Goal
- Add a new top-level folder `agent-runtime/` that contains a shared “canonical brain” (agents/skills/memory/mcp) and three runtime adapters (opencode/, aider/, pi/), plus an `agent-deck/` config area and scripts.
- Provide starter agent prompt files for 4 agents: Planner, Implementer, Reviewer, PI.
- Provide a starter shared memory pack.
- Provide starter adapter configs/placeholders for OpenCode, Aider, and Pi.
- Provide runnable shell scripts (POSIX sh) to launch each runtime and Agent Deck.
- Provide a sync script that copies shared/agents and shared/skills into each adapter folder (no symlinks) to keep it cross-platform.
- Provide README documentation explaining the architecture, how to run, and how to extend.

Constraints / notes
- Do not assume any particular OpenCode config filename beyond using a reasonable placeholder (`opencode.config.json`).
- Aider config: provide a plausible `aider.conf.yml` (or `.aider.conf.yml`) with comments; keep it minimal.
- Pi: include `AGENTS.md` and `pi-run.sh` placeholder with notes.
- Agent Deck: include `agent-deck/config.toml` placeholder and `agent-deck/worktree-setup.sh` and a `agent-deck/conductors/README.md`.
- Use safe bash/sh scripts with `set -euo pipefail` where bash is used; otherwise POSIX sh with `set -eu`.
- Keep everything under `agent-runtime/` so it doesn’t conflict with any existing repo contents.

Deliverables
- `agent-runtime/README.md`
- `agent-runtime/shared/agents/planner.md`
- `agent-runtime/shared/agents/implementer.md`
- `agent-runtime/shared/agents/reviewer.md`
- `agent-runtime/shared/agents/pi.md`
- `agent-runtime/shared/memory/README.md`
- `agent-runtime/shared/memory/scratchpad.md`
- `agent-runtime/shared/memory/decisions.md`
- `agent-runtime/shared/memory/glossary.md`
- `agent-runtime/shared/skills/README.md` plus minimal placeholder skill folders
- `agent-runtime/shared/mcp/README.md`
- `agent-runtime/opencode/README.md` + placeholder config and folders
- `agent-runtime/aider/README.md` + placeholder config and scripts
- `agent-runtime/pi/README.md` + `AGENTS.md` + scripts
- `agent-runtime/agent-deck/README.md` + `config.toml` + `worktree-setup.sh` + `conductors/README.md`
- `agent-runtime/scripts/sync-skills.sh`
- `agent-runtime/scripts/run-opencode.sh`
- `agent-runtime/scripts/run-aider.sh`
- `agent-runtime/scripts/run-pi.sh`
- `agent-runtime/scripts/launch-deck.sh`
- `agent-runtime/scripts/run-all.sh`

Make a pull request with these additions.

Repository: Brucesquared2/agent-deck

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):

User: ````

Copilot said: I can’t see **which “chat”** you mean

I can’t see which “chat” you mean from here (GitHub repos don’t expose your Copilot/OpenCode/ChatGPT chat history to me), so I can’t tell you “what chat it is in” unless you point me to where the chat is stored (a link/export/file).

What I can see from your GitHub account is that the “massive opencode library with tools and agents” most likely lives in these repos:

    Brucesquared2/opencode-typescript- (OpenCode fork; includes agents info like AGENTS.md). (github.com)
    Brucesquared2/opencode-manager (described as a mobile-first web interface to manage multiple OpenCode agents). (github.com)
    Brucesquared2/opencode-supermemory (looks OpenCode-related from the repo name/projects page). (github.com)
    Brucesquared2/opencode-background (OpenCode plugin for background tasks). (github.com)
    Brucesquared2/opencode-gemini-auth (Gemini auth plugin for OpenCode). (github.com)
    Brucesquared2/Collective-chat (multi-agent coordination tool; different project but “agents” related). (github.com)

Your mentioned repo Brucesquared2/SmartThingsPublic might be separate; I didn’t see it directly tied to “opencode” in the public search results you gave context for.
